summary: Multi-Metric Escalation Layer (Step 2) Auto-Recovery testing complete. All 29 backend tests passed. The recovery logic uses hysteresis (recovery_buffer=5) and min_clear_cycles=2 to prevent flapping. Two recovery cases implemented: Case A (no anomaly in 15min) and Case B (2+ consecutive scores < 55). RBAC enforced (guardian blocked 403). Recovery runs after escalation in scheduler. Step 1 regression tests (22) also passed - no breaking changes.

backend.update_recovery_minutes: PASS - Can update recovery_minutes via PUT
backend.update_recovery_buffer: PASS - Can update recovery_buffer via PUT
backend.update_min_clear_cycles: PASS - Can update min_clear_cycles via PUT
backend.schema_includes_recovery_fields: PASS - RULE_THRESHOLD_KEYS includes recovery_minutes, recovery_buffer, min_clear_cycles
backend.simulation_isolation: PASS - Recovery only considers is_simulated=false anomalies
backend.hysteresis_implemented: PASS - Uses recovery_buffer for hysteresis ceiling (55)
backend.min_clear_cycles_implemented: PASS - Requires min_clear_cycles consecutive low scores
backend.recovery_minutes_implemented: PASS - Case A checks recovery_minutes window
backend.case_a_logic: PASS - Case A (no anomaly in window) implemented
backend.case_b_logic: PASS - Case B (consecutive low scores) implemented
backend.scheduler_integration: PASS - Recovery runs after escalation in scheduler cycle
backend.flapping_prevention: PASS - Hysteresis prevents flapping (break on high score)
backend.resolution_metadata_case_a: PASS - Case A includes proper reason metadata
backend.resolution_metadata_case_b: PASS - Case B includes clear_cycles, ceiling info

critical_code_review_comments: Hysteresis logic correctly implemented: recovery_score_ceiling = trigger_threshold(60) - recovery_buffer(5) = 55
Case A: Resolves when no multi_metric anomaly in last recovery_minutes (15min)
Case B: Resolves when min_clear_cycles (2) consecutive scores below ceiling (55)
Flapping prevention working: Score sequence 80→50→58 does NOT resolve (58 breaks consecutive count)
Current open incident (WBAND-E2E-001) correctly NOT resolving due to flapping pattern
SSE broadcast sent to guardian on resolution
Recovery runs AFTER escalation in scheduler cycle (position 2109 vs 1677)
Recovery runs in isolated async session block
RBAC enforced - guardian blocked (403) from evaluate-recovery endpoint

success_rate.backend: 100% (29 recovery tests + 22 escalation regression tests passed)

seed_data_creation: Existing test data from previous iteration used. Open device_instability incident for WBAND-E2E-001 with flapping pattern (80→80→80→40→58) - correctly NOT resolving.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Step 2 Auto-Recovery fully tested. 29 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_instability_recovery.py cover: endpoint access, config fields, recovery logic, hysteresis, flapping prevention, audit logging, SSE broadcast, scheduler integration. One open device_instability incident exists for WBAND-E2E-001 with flapping pattern - correctly NOT resolving due to most recent score (58) being above ceiling (55). Recovery config: recovery_minutes=15, recovery_buffer=5, min_clear_cycles=2, hysteresis_ceiling=55.

recovery_cases.case_a: No anomaly in recovery_minutes window → resolve
recovery_cases.case_b: min_clear_cycles consecutive scores below ceiling → resolve

verification_summary.flapping_prevention: Score rebounds above ceiling → clear_cycles reset → NO resolution

current_open_incident.status: open (correctly NOT resolving due to flapping)
